---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 9: Physics-Informed ML in Processing
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. Title: Physics-Informed ML in Processing
- Unit role and scope.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Inject physics priors into ML workflows.

## 03. Recap from Unit 8
- Surrogate optimization context.

## 04. Why physics-informed ML
- Better extrapolation and trust.

## 05. Types of physics priors
- Constraints, invariances, simulators.

## 06. Hard vs soft constraints
- Feasibility vs penalty approaches.

## 07. Physics-informed loss terms
- Residual-based penalties.

## 08. Conservation-law examples
- Mass/energy consistency.

## 09. Symmetry and invariance encoding
- Rotation/translation handling.

## 10. Feature engineering with physics
- Dimensionless groups and scaling.

## 11. Hybrid model architectures
- Mechanistic + data-driven components.

## 12. PINN intuition (conceptual)
- PDE residual minimization.

## 13. Operator-learning preview
- Learning solution maps.

## 14. Multi-fidelity fusion
- Simulation + experiment coupling.

## 15. Data assimilation perspective
- Updating models with observations.

## 16. Uncertainty in physics-informed models
- Aleatoric + model mismatch.

## 17. Identifiability issues
- Parameter non-uniqueness.

## 18. Optimization challenges
- Stiff losses and scaling.

## 19. Constraint balancing
- Weighting data vs physics terms.

## 20. Validation strategies
- Physics metrics + prediction metrics.

## 21. OOD robustness analysis
- Shift-aware evaluation.

## 22. Failure mode: wrong physics prior
- Biased model behavior.

## 23. Failure mode: noisy boundary data
- Instability in constraints.

## 24. Failure mode: over-constrained training
- Underfit to real observations.

## 25. Mitigation strategies
- Ablations and sensitivity checks.

## 26. Human-in-the-loop checks
- Expert validation of plausibility.

## 27. Computational cost tradeoffs
- Accuracy vs runtime.

## 28. Reproducibility requirements
- Full config and solver settings.

## 29. Visualization patterns
- Residual fields and constraint violations.

## 30. Case sketch: heat-transfer-informed model
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: diffusion-constrained prediction
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: microstructure evolution hybrid
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. Integration with control loops
- Safe optimization usage.

## 34. Decision support implications
- Trust calibration.

## 35. Data requirements checklist
- What to measure for priors.

## 36. Toolchain overview
- Simulator-ML coupling workflow.

## 37. Bridge to real-time systems
- Unit 10 transition.

## 38. Exercise scaffold: define physics prior
- Constraint specification.

## 39. Exercise scaffold: train baseline vs PI model
- Comparative setup.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: evaluate constraints
- Violation and accuracy metrics.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: sensitivity study
- Prior weight sweeps.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: OOD check
- Extrapolation comparison.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: report findings
- Benefits and caveats.

## 44. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 45. Summary table
- Methods vs assumptions.

## 46. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 47. Reading assignment
- Prepare real-time ML unit.

## 48. Transition preview
- Unit 10 roadmap.

## 49. References
- Source list.

## 50. Q&A buffer slide
- Discussion and synthesis.

::: {#refs}
:::
